content: Pairing token generation in Node.js (no external dependency): Use crypto.randomBytes from node:crypto. For 6-8 char uppercase alphanumeric code (e.g. ABC-1234): const CHARS = 'ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ23456789'; // exclude O,0,I,1 lookalikes. function generatePairingCode(): string { const bytes = crypto.randomBytes(8); let code = ''; for (const b of bytes) { code += CHARS[b % CHARS.length]; if (code.length === 3) code += '-'; if (code.length >= 8) break; } return code; }. Store hash (SHA-256 or use Argon2/bcrypt — bcrypt too slow, use SHA-256 for lookup-only tokens that are already cryptographically random and short-lived). TTL: 5 min, single-use enforced by setting usedAt on exchange. Token format: 3+4 uppercase chars separated by hyphen, easy to read aloud. Alternative: use 16-byte buffer -> base32 encode -> take first 8 chars.
